title: Check Point (CHKP) Launches AI Defense Plane to Secure Agentic Workflows
content:
Check Point Software Technologies Ltd. (NASDAQ:CHKP) is one of the 8 Undervalued Stocks with Huge Upside Potential. Check Point Software Technologies Ltd. (NASDAQ:CHKP) is one of the undervalued stocks with huge upside potential. On March 23, Check Point Software announced the launch of the Check Point AI Defense Plane. This unified security control plane is designed to help enterprises govern and secure the agentic era, where AI systems transition from content generators to autonomous actors capable of accessing data and triggering workflows.

Built on Check Point’s AI Security platform, the solution integrates technologies from ThreatCloud AI and recent acquisitions of Lakera and Cyata to provide discovery, governance, and runtime control across the AI execution lifecycle. The platform’s core is an AI-native security engine that delivers adaptive protection in under 50 milliseconds across more than 100 languages.

Unlike traditional guardrails that focus solely on model safety, the AI Defense Plane secures AI behavior within live production environments. It features 3 primary modules: Workforce AI Security for employee usage governance, AI Application & Agent Security for managing permissions & data access of embedded systems, and AI Red Teaming for continuous adversarial testing of agent workflows & reasoning paths. Check Point Software Technologies Ltd. (NASDAQ:CHKP) confirmed that both the Workforce and Application modules are available immediately, while the Red Teaming module has moved into limited release.ADT Inc. (ADT) Seen as a Resilient Play with Strategic Partnerships and Recurring Revenue Strength

Check Point Software Technologies Ltd. (NASDAQ:CHKP) is a technology company that specializes in IT security. The company offers multilevel security for cloud, network, mobile, endpoints, and IoT, alongside technical support, professional services, and product certification or training.

While we acknowledge the potential of CHKP as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you're looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock.

title: How The ADT (ADT) Narrative Is Shifting As Analysts Reframe Risk And Reward
content:
Find winning stocks in any market cycle. Join 7 million investors using Simply Wall St's investing ideas for FREE.

ADT is back in focus as analysts tighten their price targets into a narrower US$7 to US$9 range, while one fair value anchor remains unchanged at US$8.10 per share. These moves reflect a reassessment of how much risk to accept as the company works through distribution changes, higher investment, and a mix of bullish and bearish views on its path ahead. Read on to see how this evolving narrative might shape the way you track ADT from here.

Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head over to our Company Report to find new ways to value ADT.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Goldman Sachs and Citi both keep Buy ratings in place while trimming targets, signaling that they still see upside potential relative to current levels even as they moderate expectations. Goldman highlights EPS and free cash flow coming in ahead of consensus in Q4, which supports the view that ADT can generate cash while it invests in its plan. Citi frames the story as delayed rather than broken, pointing to distribution optimization and subscriber acquisition actions as timing issues that push growth expectations out by a few quarters.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Barclays moves to an Underweight rating with a US$7 price target, signaling concern that the risk and reward balance has shifted less favorably at current valuation levels. Morgan Stanley cuts its target to US$7 and flags revenue and key KPI misses, along with a muted 2026 outlook that includes flat revenue and EPS guidance, which it characterizes as indicating a potentially deteriorating environment and execution risk.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!NYSE:ADT 1-Year Stock Price Chart

We've flagged 1 risk for ADT. See which could impact your investment.

What's in the News

ADT issued 2026 guidance that calls for about 20% adjusted free cash flow growth versus the prior year, with revenue and adjusted EPS described as approximately flat. The company highlighted a focus on cash flow, share repurchases, disciplined subscriber acquisition, planned growth investments, and expected tariff headwinds. The company announced a share repurchase program authorizing buybacks of up to US$1.5b of its own shares, running through April 30, 2029. ADT reported that its Board of Directors authorized a buyback plan on February 20, 2026, reinforcing its focus on returning capital to shareholders. ADT shares were added to the S&P 1000, the S&P Composite 1500, the S&P 600, and the S&P 600 Consumer Discretionary sector index, increasing its presence in key U.S. equity benchmarks.

Story Continues

How This Changes the Fair Value For ADT

Fair value remains at US$8.10 per share with no change to the core valuation anchor. Revenue growth is unchanged at about 2.46% with no adjustment to the assumed expansion rate. Net profit margin holds at about 13.72% with no revision to long-run profitability assumptions. Future P/E moves marginally from 10.72x to 10.73x in the model. The discount rate edges from 9.59% to 9.62% as the required return input is updated.

Never Miss an Update: Follow The Narrative

Narratives link a company's story, its business drivers, and its risks to a financial forecast and fair value. They update automatically as new data and research come through so you can see how the thesis is evolving.

Head over to the Simply Wall St Community and follow the Narrative on ADT to stay up to date on:

How growing use of connected smart home devices, AI automation, and the ADT+ platform feeds into subscriber growth, efficiency, and margins. The role of ADT's subscription heavy model, recurring monthly revenue, and new offerings like Trusted Neighbor and biometric smart locks in supporting cash flow and customer retention. Key risks such as high net debt near US$7.5b, pressure from lower cost DIY competitors, and signs of slower subscriber growth and underwhelming digital initiatives.
